text: The measurements here are uncertain, since the Greek gives a different account of them.

No Nabuchodonosor king of Assyria is known from other sources. Probably the story relates to some conqueror known to history under another name, but there is no agreement among scholars about his identity. We cannot, there fore, be certain what is the period in which the action of the book takes place.
‘Gessen’; this is given in the Vulgate as ‘Jesse’, probably through a copyist’s error; Gessen is mentioned in the Septuagint Greek.
Here and in the following chapters the story is told in different language and at somewhat greater length in the Septuagint Greek.
